facebook.content:
A Shift in Your Authority

Do you ever feel like the circumstances of your life dictate your emotions and decisions? The Bible says, "You did not choose Me, but I chose you." (John 15:16) What if your authority wasn't in your circumstances, but in your identity as a chosen child of God?

When you recognize your authority is in Christ, you'll no longer be controlled by the things around you. You'll be free to walk in the authority and power He's given you.

instagram.content:
"You know you're not in the position of needing God's help when you're in a situation, but when you're asking for His guidance. The difference is in the language we use. 

Proverbs 3:5-6 says, "Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight."

tiktok.content: You trying to build a life based on what others think you should be, rather than who God says you are? "Do not call anyone on earth 'father,' for you have one Father..." (Matthew 23:9) Your identity is not in who you married or what degree you hold. Your identity is in who God says you are.
